overview: Survive the early game by answering every threat, win the resource war with card advantage, and close with a small number of hard-to-kill finishers. Time is your ally — trade efficiently, do not panic, and only commit a threat once the board is safe.
win_conditions: A resilient finisher deployed after the opponent is out of pressure
Decking or grinding the opponent out once you control the game

keep_criteria: Three or four lands with a mix of cheap removal and counterspells
Early interaction to survive to your powerful late game
Card draw or card selection to find missing pieces
mulligan_criteria: No early interaction against an unknown, likely-aggressive field
All finishers and no way to survive to cast them
Land-light hands that cannot answer threats on curve

examples.reason: Lands, two cheap answers, and a card-draw spell can survive and stabilize.
Two finishers and no interaction dies long before turn six.

early_game: Answer only real threats; let irrelevant permanents resolve
Hold up interaction every turn — do not tap out without a reason
Develop lands and survive; you do not need to do anything proactive
mid_game: Use sweepers when the opponent has overcommitted for maximum value
Refill with card draw once the board is stable
Counter the spells that matter; let harmless ones through
late_game: Deploy a finisher only when you can protect it or the board is clear
Close the game promptly — every extra turn gives the opponent draws

key_cards.name: Cheap removal
key_cards.notes: Trade up on mana against early threats to survive to the late game.

key_cards.name: Board wipes
key_cards.notes: Wait for the opponent to overcommit; one good sweep can win the game.

key_cards.name: Card draw
key_cards.notes: Convert a stable board into an overwhelming card-advantage lead.

key_cards.name: Finisher
key_cards.notes: Few copies; deploy only when safe so it actually ends the game.

sequencing_tips: Leave interaction mana open; pass with counterspell mana up when unsure
Hold sweepers until the opponent commits enough to make them worth it
Sequence card draw on the opponent's end step to keep mana for answers

matchups.advice: Prioritize cheap removal and sweepers; stabilize life total, then take over.
Shift to counterspells and disruption; identify and answer their key piece.

common_threats: Fast aggro that empties your life total before you stabilize
Combo decks that win without attacking
Resilient threats and planeswalkers your removal cannot cleanly answer
